title: magnolia fraseri subsp. pyramidata
intro: Magnolia fraseri subsp. pyramidata, also known as the Fraser magnolia or pyramidata magnolia, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to the southeastern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant are believed to include antioxidant, anti-cancer, and anti-diabetic properties, as well as the ability to soothe digestive issues and alleviate skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Magnolia fraseri subsp. pyramidata include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
